# 📝 KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank

---

## 📘 PART A — Short-Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

> **[KTU University Exam — June 2023]**
> **Q1. Define Multi-Color Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (M-FISH). Mention any two advantages of M-FISH over conventional G-banding.**

**Model Answer:**

**M-FISH (Multi-Color Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization)** is a molecular cytogenetic technique in which a set of whole-chromosome painting probes — each labeled with a spectrally distinct fluorochrome (or a unique combinatorial ratio of fluorochromes) — is hybridized **simultaneously** onto a single metaphase spread, enabling the differential identification of **all 24 human chromosomes (22 autosomes + X + Y) in one hybridization reaction**.

**Two advantages over G-banding:**

1. **Higher resolution of structural rearrangements** — M-FISH detects translocations and marker chromosomes that are too small or subtle to be resolved by G-banding (especially cryptic translocations in cancer karyotypes).
2. **Speed and objectivity** — M-FISH analysis is semi-automated (spectral classification algorithm), reducing the subjectivity inherent in visual band-by-band comparison, and allows simultaneous karyotyping of all chromosomes in a single experiment.

> **[KTU University Exam — December 2022]**
> **Q2. What is Chromosome In Situ Suppression (CISS)? Why is Cot-1 DNA added during M-FISH probe hybridization?**

**Model Answer:**

**Chromosome In Situ Suppression (CISS)** is a technique used during FISH and M-FISH to suppress the non-specific hybridization of **repetitive DNA sequences** (e.g., Alu elements, LINEs) present within chromosome painting probes.

**Why Cot-1 DNA is added:**
Chromosome painting probes are derived from whole-chromosome DNA libraries, which contain abundant **interspersed repetitive sequences** in addition to chromosome-specific unique sequences. If these repeats are not blocked, they will hybridize non-specifically to all chromosomes in the metaphase spread, producing a high background and reducing signal specificity.

**Cot-1 DNA** is a preparation of **highly repetitive, rapidly re-annealing genomic DNA** (enriched for Alu, LINE, and other short interspersed repeats). It is added to the hybridization mixture at **10–50× the concentration of the painting probe** and pre-anneals to the repetitive sequences within the probes, thereby **competitively blocking** their non-specific binding to chromosomal repeats. This ensures that only the **unique, chromosome-specific sequences** of the painting probe hybridize, producing clean, chromosome-specific fluorescent signals.

---

## 📕 PART B — Long-Answer Questions (14 Marks Each)

> **[KTU University Exam — May 2024, Module 4, Internal Choice Question]**

### ❓ Question A (14 Marks)

> **(a)** Describe the **combinatorial labeling principle** used in M-FISH. With the help of a suitable mathematical expression, justify why **5 fluorophores** are sufficient to identify all 24 human chromosomes. **(7 Marks)**
>
> **(b)** Explain the **step-by-step protocol** of M-FISH, including slide preparation, probe hybridization, post-hybridization washes, image acquisition, and spectral classification. Mention the role of **DAPI counterstain** and **Cot-1 DNA**. **(7 Marks)**

---

### ✅ Model Answer — Question A

#### (a) Combinatorial Labeling Principle (7 Marks)

In M-FISH, each whole-chromosome painting probe is labeled with a **unique combination** of $n$ spectrally distinct fluorophores. The labeling can be represented in **binary form**: each fluorophore is either present (1) or absent (0) in a given probe.

**Total number of distinct binary combinations** for $n$ fluorophores:

$$N_{\text{total}} = 2^n$$

**Subtracting the degenerate all-zero code** (no fluorophore = no signal):

$$N_{\text{usable}} = 2^n - 1$$

**For $n = 5$** (the standard M-FISH set: Spectrum Aqua, FITC, Cy3, Texas Red, Cy5):

$$N_{\text{usable}} = 2^5 - 1 = 32 - 1 = 31$$

Since $31 \geq 24$ (the number of human chromosomes: 22 autosomes + X + Y), 5 fluorophores provide a **theoretical capacity of 31 unique color codes**, which is more than sufficient to identify all 24 chromosomes uniquely.

**Justification of the minimum:** To determine the theoretical minimum $n_{\min}$ for 24 chromosomes:

$$n_{\min} = \lceil \log_2(24 + 1) \rceil = \lceil \log_2(25) \rceil = \lceil 4.644 \rceil = 5$$

Thus, **5 fluorophores is the minimum practical number** for human M-FISH. (1 Mark for derivation + 1 Mark for binary table explanation + 1 Mark for substitution + 1 Mark for n_min formula + 2 Marks for example assignment + 1 Mark for conclusion)

#### (b) M-FISH Protocol (7 Marks)

**Step 1 — Slide Preparation (1 Mark):**
- Peripheral blood lymphocytes are cultured in RPMI medium with **phytohemagglutinin (PHA)** for 72 hours to stimulate mitosis.
- **Colchicine** (0.1 µg/mL, 30 min) is added to arrest cells at metaphase.
- Cells are treated with **hypotonic KCl (0.075 M, 37°C, 15 min)** to swell the nuclei.
- **Fixation** in 3:1 methanol:acetic acid; cells are dropped onto cleaned glass slides and aged.

**Step 2 — Probe Preparation and Hybridization (2 Marks):**
- A **cocktail of 24 whole-chromosome painting probes** is prepared, each labeled with a unique 5-fluorophore binary code.
- **Cot-1 DNA** (10–50× excess) is added to **suppress repetitive sequences** (CISS).
- The slide is denatured in **70% formamide / 2× SSC at 70°C for 2 min**.
- The probe is denatured at **75°C for 5 min**.
- **Co-hybridization** is performed at **37°C for 16–48 hours** in a humidified chamber.

**Step 3 — Post-Hybridization Washes (1 Mark):**
- **Stringent washes** in 50% formamide / 2× SSC at 42°C (3 × 5 min) remove weakly bound probes.
- Final wash in 2× SSC at 42°C.
- Slides are mounted in **antifade medium containing DAPI (4',6-diamidino-2-phenylindole)**.

**Role of DAPI Counterstain (1 Mark):**
DAPI binds to AT-rich regions of DNA and emits blue fluorescence. It is used as a **counterstain** that provides a **G-band-like banding pattern**, allowing the cytogeneticist to correlate the M-FISH pseudocolor assignment with the standard chromosome morphology and confirm correct chromosome identification.

**Step 4 — Image Acquisition (1 Mark):**
- An **epifluorescence microscope** equipped with an **automated 6-position filter wheel** and a **cooled CCD camera** is used.
- For each metaphase field, **5 monochrome images** (one per fluorophore) are captured sequentially, plus 1 DAPI image.

**Step 5 — Spectral Classification (1 Mark):**
- M-FISH software performs **pixel-by-pixel spectral unmixing**, computing the **Euclidean distance** between each pixel's intensity vector and a reference signature library for all 24 chromosomes.
- Each pixel is **assigned to the closest chromosome** (minimum-distance classification), and the chromosome is rendered in a **pseudocolor**.
- The result is a **classified pseudocolor karyotype** for cytogenetic interpretation.

---

### ❓ Question B (14 Marks) — Alternative Choice

> **(a)** What is **Spectral Karyotyping (SKY)**? Compare SKY and M-FISH in terms of instrumentation, fluorophore detection method, and clinical applicability. **(7 Marks)**
>
> **(b)** Discuss the **applications of M-FISH** in (i) cancer cytogenetics, (ii) prenatal diagnosis, and (iii) radiation biodosimetry. For each application, specify one example chromosomal abnormality detected. **(7 Marks)**

---

### ✅ Model Answer — Question B

#### (a) Spectral Karyotyping (SKY) vs. M-FISH (7 Marks)

**Spectral Karyotyping (SKY)** is a variant of M-FISH developed by Schröck et al. (1996) that uses an **interferometer-based spectral imaging system** to capture the **complete emission spectrum** (typically 400–800 nm at ~10 nm resolution) for every pixel in a single exposure, rather than acquiring discrete filter-based channels sequentially.

**Comparison Table (5 Marks for the table, 2 Marks for explanation):**

| Parameter | M-FISH | SKY |
|---|---|---|
| **Optical Detection** | 5 (or 6) bandpass filter sets in an automated filter wheel | Interferometer + CCD — captures full emission spectrum in one shot |
| **Number of Fluorophores** | 5 (binary combinatorial: 31 codes) | 5 (combinatorial, same as M-FISH) |
| **Acquisition Mode** | Sequential — one fluorophore at a time | Single exposure, all fluorophores simultaneously |
| **Acquisition Speed** | Slower (sequential filter changes) | Faster (one exposure) |
| **Photobleaching** | Higher (multiple exposures per field) | Lower (single exposure) |
| **Spectral Crosstalk Correction** | None (assumed) | Built-in (spectral unmixing via Fourier transform) |
| **Instrumentation Cost** | Lower (standard epifluorescence + filter wheel) | Higher (requires Sagnac interferometer) |
| **Clinical Adoption** | Widespread (most clinical labs use M-FISH) | Limited to research / specialized centers |
| **Sensitivity to Chromosome Overlaps** | Lower (spectral overlap can cause misclassification) | Higher (full spectrum unmixing resolves overlaps) |

**Explanation (2 Marks):**
While both M-FISH and SKY use **combinatorial fluorophore labeling** with 5 fluorophores to identify 24 chromosomes, they differ fundamentally in the **image acquisition philosophy**. M-FISH captures each fluorophore channel through a **discrete bandpass filter** and relies on software to combine them. SKY uses a **sagnac interferometer + CCD** to capture the **full emission spectrum** at once, and applies **spectral unmixing** (Fourier transform-based) to deconvolve the contributions of each fluorochrome. SKY has higher spectral resolution and can resolve chromosomes that overlap, but its cost limits its routine clinical use.

#### (b) Applications of M-FISH (7 Marks)

**Application 1: Cancer Cytogenetics (2.5 Marks):**
M-FISH is invaluable for characterizing **complex karyotypes** in cancer cells, where multiple translocations and marker chromosomes accumulate. Traditional G-banding often fails to identify the **origin of marker chromosomes** in tumors with >3–4 rearrangements.

- **Example:** Detection of the **Philadelphia chromosome — t(9;22)(q34;q11)** in **Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML)**. M-FISH simultaneously reveals any **additional translocations** (e.g., variant Ph translocations involving chromosomes 1, 8, 11, 17) that would be missed by single-probe FISH or G-banding.

**Application 2: Prenatal Diagnosis (2.5 Marks):**
M-FISH can be used on **uncultured amniocytes or chorionic villus samples** to detect **aneuploidies** of any chromosome within 24–48 hours, providing a faster alternative to traditional karyotyping (which requires 7–14 days of cell culture).

- **Example:** Rapid detection of **Trisomy 21 (Down syndrome)** by M-FISH on amniotic fluid cells. All three copies of chromosome 21 are simultaneously visualized in the same pseudocolor in a single hybridization.

**Application 3: Radiation Biodosimetry (2 Marks):**
M-FISH detects **dicentric chromosomes, ring chromosomes, and translocations** in peripheral blood lymphocytes of individuals exposed to ionizing radiation. The frequency of these aberrations is **dose-dependent**, allowing estimation of absorbed dose.

- **Example:** Quantification of **dicentric chromosomes in lymphocytes** of radiation workers following accidental exposure. M-FISH enables **unambiguous identification of the chromosomes involved** in the dicentric, improving the accuracy of dose reconstruction.

---

## ⚠️ KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Student Mistakes in M-FISH Exam Questions (Costing 2–4 Marks Each):**
>
> 1. **Confusing M-FISH with conventional FISH:** Students often write that M-FISH uses "one fluorochrome per chromosome" — this is **wrong**. M-FISH uses **combinatorial fluorophore ratios** to generate unique color codes. The whole point of M-FISH is **multiplexing beyond the number of available fluorophores**.
>
> 2. **Forgetting Cot-1 DNA / CISS:** When asked about the protocol, students list hybridization steps but **omit Cot-1 DNA**. Examiners specifically test the understanding of **repetitive sequence suppression**. Always mention CISS in any M-FISH protocol question.
>
> 3. **Wrong formula for multiplexing capacity:** Students write $N = 2^n$ (forgetting the $-1$ for the all-zero code). The correct formula is $N = 2^n - 1$. This is worth **2 full marks** in a 7-mark question.
>
> 4. **Confusing SKY and M-FISH:** SKY uses a **single full-spectrum acquisition via interferometer**, NOT filter-based sequential imaging. Writing "SKY uses 5 filters" will lose 3 marks.
>
> 5. **Omitting the DAPI counterstain role:** DAPI is **not just a counterstain** — it provides **banding-like morphological confirmation** of chromosome identity. Students who say "DAPI stains the chromosomes blue" without elaborating lose 1 mark.
>
> 6. **Ignoring the threshold step in classification:** When describing the spectral algorithm, students forget the **threshold $T$** for unclassified pixels. Examiners reward mentioning this, as it shows understanding of **classification uncertainty**.

---

## 🔁 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **High-Density Rapid Revision Checklist for M-FISH (KTU 2024 Module 4):**

### 🧬 Core Concepts
- ☐ M-FISH = simultaneous identification of **all 24 human chromosomes** via **combinatorial fluorophore labeling**
- ☐ Standard fluorophore set: **Spectrum Aqua, FITC, Cy3, Texas Red, Cy5** ($n = 5$)
- ☐ Multiplexing capacity formula: $\boxed{N = 2^n - 1 = 31}$ for 5 fluorophores
- ☐ M-FISH can identify **complex translocations, marker chromosomes, aneuploidies, cryptic rearrangements** that G-banding misses

### 🔬 Protocol Essentials
- ☐ Cell arrest: **Colchicine (0.1 µg/mL, 30 min)**
- ☐ Hypotonic: **0.075 M KCl, 37°C, 15 min**
- ☐ Probe cocktail: **24 WCP probes + Cot-1 DNA + hybridization buffer**
- ☐ Denaturation: **Slide 70°C / 2 min in 70% formamide; Probe 75°C / 5 min**
- ☐ Hybridization: **37°C, 16–48 hours**
- ☐ Stringent wash: **50% formamide / 2× SSC, 42°C**
- ☐ Counterstain: **DAPI** in antifade medium

### 📐 Mathematical Tools
- ☐ Minimum fluorophores for $K$ chromosomes: $n_{\min} = \lceil \log_2(K+1) \rceil$
- ☐ Spectral classification: $d_{p,c} = \sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^{5}(I_{p,i} - R_{c,i})^2}$
- ☐ Beer-Lambert: $A = \varepsilon c l$; Fluorescence: $I_F \approx I_0 \cdot \Phi_F \cdot \varepsilon \ln(10) \cdot c \cdot l$
- ☐ Resolution: $R = 0.61 \lambda / NA \approx 250$ nm (≈ 2–5 Mb for metaphase chromosomes)

### 🧪 Key Reagents & Their Roles
- ☐ **Cot-1 DNA** → blocks repetitive sequences (CISS)
- ☐ **DAPI** → counterstain + banding reference
- ☐ **Formamide** → denaturant in hybridization + washes
- ☐ **Colchicine** → microtubule inhibitor, arrests at metaphase
- ☐ **PHA** → mitogen that stimulates lymphocyte division

### 🔄 SKY vs. M-FISH Distinction
- ☐ **M-FISH** → filter-based, **sequential** acquisition
- ☐ **SKY** → interferometer-based, **single full-spectrum** acquisition
- ☐ Both use 5 fluorophores and combinatorial labeling

### 🏥 Top 3 Applications (Memorize)
- ☐ **Cancer cytogenetics** (complex karyotypes, marker chromosomes)
- ☐ **Prenatal diagnosis** (rapid 24-chromosome aneuploidy screening)
- ☐ **Radiation biodosimetry** (dicentric + translocation scoring)

### ⚠️ Examiner Traps to Avoid
- ☐ Never write $N = 2^n$ — always $N = 2^n - 1$
- ☐ Never say "one fluorochrome per chromosome" for M-FISH
- ☐ Never omit Cot-1 DNA in protocol descriptions
- ☐ Never confuse SKY's interferometer with M-FISH's filter wheel

<!-- SECTION_5_END -->

topics.title: Fluorescence in situ hybridization detection of HER2 amplification
topics.content:
> **APJ ABDUL KALAM TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY (KTU) | 2024 SCHEME**
> - **Course:** PEBB861 - CYTOGENETICS
> - **Module:** Module Module 4
> - **Topic:** Fluorescence in situ hybridization detection of HER2 amplification

<!-- SECTION_1_START -->
# Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) for HER2 Amplification Detection

## 1. Core Technical Definition

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **FISH (Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization)** is a molecular cytogenetic technique that uses fluorescently labeled DNA probes to detect and localize specific DNA sequences on chromosomes, in cells, or in tissue sections. In oncology, **FISH-based detection of HER2 amplification** is the **gold-standard confirmatory assay** used to determine the copy number status of the *ERBB2* (HER2/neu) gene located on **chromosome 17q12**, primarily in breast and gastric carcinomas.

**Full Form & Gene Locus:**
- **HER2** = Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2
- Gene Symbol: *ERBB2* (v-erb-b2 erythroblastic leukemia viral oncogene homolog 2)
- Cytogenetic Locus: **17q12**
- Encodes a **185 kDa transmembrane tyrosine kinase receptor** of the EGFR family.

### Intuitive Analogy (Plain English)

> [!NOTE]
> **Think of a chromosome as a long library shelf, and each gene as a specific book.** In some cancers, the cell **photocopies** the "HER2 book" hundreds of times, gluing the copies onto the shelf. **FISH is like sending in a glowing fluorescent "highlighter"** that sticks ONLY to the HER2 book. When we shine a special blue light, every copy of the HER2 book lights up like a tiny green/orange dot. By counting the dots inside a single cell, we can tell whether HER2 has been **amplified** (too many copies = cancer driver) or is **normal** (just 2 copies, like a normal book on a shelf).

### Key Standard Metrics (ASCO/CAP 2018 Guidelines)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> - **HER2/CEP17 Ratio Threshold for Positive:** **≥ 2.0**
> - **Average HER2 Copy Number (Positive):** **≥ 4.0 signals/cell** (with ratio <2.0)
> - **Negative:** Ratio < 2.0 AND average HER2 copy number < 4.0/cell
> - **Minimum cells scored:** **20** (preferably 40–60 non-overlapping tumor nuclei)
> - **Probe Pair:** Orange/Red (HER2 LSI) + Green (CEP17)

---

## 2. Why HER2 FISH Matters

| Aspect | Significance |
|---|---|
| **Prognosis** | HER2-amplified tumors are biologically aggressive |
| **Predictive** | Predicts response to **Trastuzumab (Herceptin®)**, **Pertuzumab**, **T-DM1 (Ado-trastuzumab emtansine)**, **Lapatinib** |
| **Disease Site** | Invasive breast carcinoma (≈15–20% amplified), Gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma (≈7–34%) |
| **Companion Diagnostic** | FDA-approved companion test for targeted therapy |
<!-- SECTION_1_END -->

<!-- SECTION_2_START -->
# Deep Theoretical Analysis

## 2.1 The Two-Probe (Dual-Color) FISH Strategy

HER2 status is never reported from HER2 alone — it is always **co-hybridized with a chromosome 17 centromere (CEP17)** enumeration probe. This is critical because:

- **HER2 amplification** can occur via:
  1. **Focal gene amplification** (extra copies of 17q12 only) → Ratio ↑↑
  2. **Polysomy 17** (extra entire chromosome 17) → Both probes ↑, ratio near 1
  3. **Co-amplification** (both mechanisms combined)

Hence, **ratio + copy number** must always be reported together.

> [!NOTE]
> **LSI HER2 Probe** = Orange/Red signal, spans the *ERBB2* locus at 17q12 (≈190 kb region).
> **CEP17 Probe** = Green/Aqua signal, binds the **alpha-satellite repeat** of chromosome 17 centromere (17p11.1–q11.1).

## 2.2 Stepwise Molecular Workflow

1. **Tissue Preparation**
   - Source: Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded (FFPE) tissue or fresh/frozen.
   - 4–5 µm thick sections mounted on **positively charged slides**.
2. **Deparaffinization** — Xylene + graded ethanol.
3. **Protease Digestion** — Pepsin/HCl to expose DNA.
4. **Denaturation** — Heat to **72 °C for ~5 min** (co-denatures probe + target).
5. **Hybridization** — 37 °C overnight (16–18 hours).
6. **Stringent Washes** — 0.4× SSC / 0.3% NP-40 at 72 °C.
7. **Counterstain** — DAPI (4',6-diamidino-2-phenylindole) for nuclear visualization.
8. **Visualization** — Epifluorescence microscope with **DAPI, Orange, and Aqua/Green filter sets**.

## 2.3 Scoring Algorithm (ASCO/CAP 2018 — Breast Cancer)

| HER2/CEP17 Ratio | Avg HER2 Copy # / Cell | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| **≥ 2.0** | Any | **POSITIVE** (amplified) |
| **< 2.0** | **≥ 6.0** | **POSITIVE** |
| **< 2.0** | **≥ 4.0 and < 6.0** | **EQUIVOCAL** — recount by another scorer, or reflex IHC |
| **< 2.0** | **< 4.0** | **NEGATIVE** |

## 2.4 KTU High-Yield Formula Sheet

| Symbol/Metric | Definition / Formula | Normal Value | Positive Threshold |
|---|---|---|---|
| **$R$** | HER2/CEP17 ratio $= \dfrac{\text{Total HER2 signals counted}}{\text{Total CEP17 signals counted}}$ | < 2.0 | **≥ 2.0** |
| **$\bar{C}_{HER2}$** | Average HER2 copy number per cell $= \dfrac{\sum_{i=1}^{N} HER2_i}{N}$ | ≈ 2.0 | **≥ 4.0** (equivocal 4.0–5.9) |
| **$\bar{C}_{CEP17}$** | Average CEP17 per cell (polysomy indicator) | ≈ 2.0 | ≥ 3.0 = Polysomy 17 |
| **$N$** | Number of tumor nuclei scored | — | **≥ 20** (40–60 preferred) |
| **$h_i$** | HER2 signal count in nucleus $i$ | integer | — |
| **$c_i$** | CEP17 signal count in nucleus $i$ | integer | — |
| **$S_{HER2}$** | Total HER2 signals $= \sum_{i=1}^{N} h_i$ | — | — |
| **$S_{CEP17}$** | Total CEP17 signals $= \sum_{i=1}^{N} c_i$ | — | — |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Always report **BOTH** the ratio AND the average HER2 copy number per cell. A single number is non-compliant with ASCO/CAP 2018.

## 2.5 Real-World Engineering & Clinical Utility

- **Companion Diagnostics Industry:** Drives the multi-billion-dollar market of personalized oncology.
- **Image Cytometry:** Software algorithms (e.g., BioView, MetaSystems) automate signal counting using **segmentation and object detection**.
- **Optical Engineering:** Multi-bandpass filter cubes, narrow-band LED excitation, low-noise CCD/CMOS sensors with high quantum yield in the **green (520 nm)**, **orange (560 nm)**, and **aqua (425 nm)** emission peaks.
<!-- SECTION_2_END -->

<!-- SECTION_3_START -->
# Step-by-Step Derivations & Analytical Matrix

## 3.1 Mathematical Derivation: HER2/CEP17 Ratio

Let us formally derive the scoring metrics. Consider $N$ non-overlapping tumor nuclei scored in the invasive component of the carcinoma.

$$
S_{HER2} \;=\; \sum_{i=1}^{N} h_i
$$

$$
S_{CEP17} \;=\; \sum_{i=1}^{N} c_i
$$

The **HER2/CEP17 ratio** for the population is defined as:

$$
R \;=\; \frac{S_{HER2}}{S_{CEP17}} \;=\; \frac{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{N} h_i}{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{N} c_i}
$$

The **average HER2 copy number per cell** is:

$$
\bar{C}_{HER2} \;=\; \frac{S_{HER2}}{N} \;=\; \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N} h_i
$$

The **average CEP17 copy number per cell** is:

$$
\bar{C}_{CEP17} \;=\; \frac{S_{CEP17}}{N} \;=\; \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N} c_i
$$

> [!NOTE]
> **Conversion logic:** $R$ is a *population ratio* (total signals over total signals) — not the mean of per-cell ratios. The mean of ratios is mathematically biased upward when CEP17 is small. ASCO/CAP mandates the **population-ratio form** $R = S_{HER2}/S_{CEP17}$.

## 3.2 Worked Numerical Example

Suppose a pathologist scores **40 tumor nuclei**. The per-cell HER2 ($h_i$) and CEP17 ($c_i$) signals in the first six nuclei are:

| Nucleus $i$ | HER2 ($h_i$) | CEP17 ($c_i$) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 8 | 2 |
| 2 | 12 | 3 |
| 3 | 10 | 2 |
| 4 | 6 | 2 |
| 5 | 14 | 2 |
| 6 | 9 | 3 |
| ... (i = 7 to 40) | ... | ... |
| **Total** | $S_{HER2} = 360$ | $S_{CEP17} = 84$ |

**Step 1 — Compute the population ratio:**

$$
R \;=\; \frac{S_{HER2}}{S_{CEP17}} \;=\; \frac{360}{84} \;\approx\; 4.29
$$

**Step 2 — Compute average HER2 copy number per cell:**

$$
\bar{C}_{HER2} \;=\; \frac{S_{HER2}}{N} \;=\; \frac{360}{40} \;=\; 9.0 \;\text{signals/cell}
$$

**Step 3 — Compute average CEP17 per cell:**

$$
\bar{C}_{CEP17} \;=\; \frac{S_{CEP17}}{N} \;=\; \frac{84}{40} \;=\; 2.1 \;\text{signals/cell}
$$

**Step 4 — Apply ASCO/CAP 2018 decision rule:**

$$
R = 4.29 \;\geq\; 2.0 \;\;\Longrightarrow\;\; \boxed{\text{HER2 POSITIVE (amplified)}}
$$

Even without consulting $\bar{C}_{HER2}$, the ratio alone classifies this case as positive. The patient becomes eligible for **trastuzumab-based therapy**.

## 3.3 Analytical Decision Matrix (Full Algorithmic Resolution)

$$
\text{Decision}(R,\bar{C}_{HER2}) \;=\;
\begin{cases}
\text{POSITIVE}, & R \geq 2.0 \\[4pt]
\text{POSITIVE}, & R < 2.0 \;\wedge\; \bar{C}_{HER2} \geq 6.0 \\[4pt]
\text{EQUIVOCAL}, & R < 2.0 \;\wedge\; 4.0 \leq \bar{C}_{HER2} < 6.0 \\[4pt]
\text{NEGATIVE}, & R < 2.0 \;\wedge\; \bar{C}_{HER2} < 4.0
\end{cases}
$$

## 3.4 Handling Heterogeneity & the "Recount" Rule

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Heterogeneity Rule:** If ≥ 5% but < 50% of tumor cells show amplification, the case is reported as **"Heterogeneous for HER2 amplification"** alongside the overall result. If ≥ 50% amplified, report the amplified population as the dominant pattern.

**Equivocal recount procedure** (for $4.0 \leq \bar{C}_{HER2} < 6.0$ with $R < 2.0$):

1. Have a **second independent technologist** recount ≥ 20 additional cells.
2. Recalculate $R$ and $\bar{C}_{HER2}$.
3. If still equivocal → perform **IHC reflex test** on the same block.
<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
# Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 End-to-End FISH Workflow Block Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[FFPE Tumor Block] --> B[Sectioning 4-5 micrometer]
    B --> C[Mount on Positively Charged Slide]
    C --> D[Deparaffinization Xylene + Graded Ethanol]
    D --> E[Protease Digestion Pepsin HCl 37C]
    E --> F[Denaturation 72C 5 min]
    F --> G[Co-denature LSI HER2 Orange + CEP17 Green Probe]
    G --> H[Hybridization 37C Overnight 16-18 h]
    H --> I[Stringent Wash 0.4x SSC 72C]
    I --> J[DAPI Counterstain]
    J --> K[Epifluorescence Microscopy]
    K --> L[Score 20-60 Tumor Nuclei]
    L --> M[Compute R and CHER2]
    M --> N{Decision Rule}
    N -- Ratio >= 2.0 --> O[POSITIVE]
    N -- Ratio < 2.0 AND CHER2 >= 6.0 --> O
    N -- Ratio < 2.0 AND 4.0 <= CHER2 < 6.0 --> P[EQUIVOCAL Recount or IHC Reflex]
    N -- Ratio < 2.0 AND CHER2 < 4.0 --> Q[NEGATIVE]
    O --> R[Eligible for Trastuzumab Therapy]
    P --> S[Reflex IHC HER2]
    Q --> T[Standard Chemotherapy]
```

## 4.2 Probe-Binding Architecture (Conceptual Chromosome View)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph CellNucleus["Tumor Cell Nucleus DAPI Blue Stain"]
        direction TB
        Ch17a["Chromosome 17 Copy 1"]
        Ch17b["Chromosome 17 Copy 2"]
        Ch17a --> S1a["CEP17 GREEN Signal 1"]
        Ch17a --> H1a["HER2 ORANGE Cluster Normal 2 dots"]
        Ch17b --> S1b["CEP17 GREEN Signal 2"]
        Ch17b --> H1b["HER2 ORANGE Cluster Normal 2 dots"]
    end
    Legend1["GREEN Dot = CEP17 alpha-satellite 17p11.1-q11.1"]
    Legend2["ORANGE Dot = HER2 LSI 17q12 ERBB2 locus"]
```

## 4.3 ASCO/CAP 2018 Decision Tree (Standalone View)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start[Score N >= 20 Tumor Nuclei] --> ComputeR[Compute R = S_HER2 / S_CEP17]
    ComputeR --> ComputeC[Compute C_HER2 = S_HER2 / N]
    ComputeR --> Q1{R greater or equal 2.0}
    Q1 -- YES --> POS1[HER2 POSITIVE Amplified]
    Q1 -- NO --> Q2{C_HER2 greater or equal 6.0}
    Q2 -- YES --> POS2[HER2 POSITIVE High Polysomy Pattern]
    Q2 -- NO --> Q3{C_HER2 between 4.0 and 6.0}
    Q3 -- YES --> EQUIV[HER2 EQUIVOCAL Recount or IHC]
    Q3 -- NO --> NEG[HER2 NEGATIVE]
    POS1 --> Rx1[Trastuzumab Eligible]
    POS2 --> Rx2[Trastuzumab Eligible]
    EQUIV --> Rx3[Reflex IHC HER2 on Same Block]
    NEG --> Rx4[Endocrine or Standard Chemo]
```

## 4.4 Signal Pattern Gallery — What You See Under the Microscope

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[HER2 FISH Signal Patterns] --> B1[Pattern 1 NORMAL]
    A --> B2[Pattern 2 FOCAL AMPLIFICATION]
    A --> B3[Pattern 3 POLYSOMY 17]
    A --> B4[Pattern 4 DOUBLE MINUTES]
    B1 --> D1["2 Green CEP17 + 2 Orange HER2 | Ratio near 1.0 | NEGATIVE"]
    B2 --> D2["2 Green CEP17 + 10 to 20 Orange HER2 clusters | Ratio greater or equal 2.0 | POSITIVE"]
    B3 --> D3["5 to 8 Green CEP17 + 5 to 8 Orange HER2 | Ratio near 1.0 | NEGATIVE polysomy"]
    B4 --> D4["2 Green CEP17 + numerous scattered Orange HER2 dots diffuse | Ratio greater or equal 2.0 | POSITIVE"]
```
<!-- SECTION_4_END -->

<!-- SECTION_5_START -->
# KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

---

## Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### **Q1. [KTU University Exam – Model Question, Cytogenetics Module 4]**
**Define FISH. Mention the two probes used in HER2 FISH testing and the chromosome on which the HER2 gene is located.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

**FISH (Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization)** is a molecular cytogenetic technique that uses fluorescently labeled DNA or RNA probes to detect specific DNA sequences directly inside cells, chromosomes, or tissue sections under a fluorescence microscope.

The two probes used in HER2 FISH are:
1. **LSI HER2 (orange/red)** — a locus-specific identifier probe targeting the *ERBB2* gene at **17q12**.
2. **CEP17 (green/aqua)** — a chromosome enumeration probe binding the alpha-satellite centromeric repeat of **chromosome 17**.

The HER2 gene is located on the **long arm of chromosome 17, region 1, band 2 (17q12)**. *(3 marks: definition 1, probes 1, locus 1)*

---

### **Q2. [KTU University Exam – Model Question, Cytogenetics Module 4]**
**State the ASCO/CAP 2018 criteria for calling a breast carcinoma HER2-positive by FISH.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

According to the **ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines**, a breast carcinoma is reported as **HER2-positive** by FISH if **either** of the following criteria is met:

1. **HER2/CEP17 ratio ≥ 2.0** (regardless of average HER2 copy number), **OR**
2. **HER2/CEP17 ratio < 2.0** but **average HER2 copy number ≥ 6.0 signals/cell**.

A minimum of **20 non-overlapping tumor nuclei** must be scored in the invasive component. *(3 marks: criteria 1, criteria 2, scoring requirement)*

---

## Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each, Internal Choice)

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall:** Students frequently make these errors and lose 2–3 marks per answer:
> 1. Reporting **only the ratio** without the **average copy number per cell** — non-compliant with ASCO/CAP 2018.
> 2. Confusing **HER2 (LSI) probe color (orange/red)** with **CEP17 (green)** — examiners deduct for swapping colors.
> 3. Forgetting the **scoring cell count minimum (≥ 20)** in the algorithm.
> 4. Reporting **IHC score** when the question is about **FISH** — they are two different modalities.

---

### **Question A (14 marks) — Option 1**

#### (a) Describe in detail the principle and step-by-step procedure of Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FISH) for detection of HER2 amplification in a breast carcinoma tissue sample. **(7 marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**Principle (2 marks):**
FISH uses **denatured, fluorescently labeled single-stranded DNA probes** that hybridize (by complementary base pairing) to specific target DNA sequences inside the morphologically preserved nucleus of a tumor cell. For HER2 detection, two probes are co-hybridized:
- **LSI HER2 (orange)** → binds the *ERBB2* locus at **17q12**.
- **CEP17 (green)** → binds the centromeric alpha-satellite of chromosome 17.

Counting the **ratio of HER2 (orange) signals to CEP17 (green) signals** within tumor nuclei determines whether the *ERBB2* gene is amplified.

**Step-by-Step Procedure (5 marks):**

1. **Tissue selection and sectioning** — 4–5 µm sections of FFPE invasive breast carcinoma tissue on positively charged slides. *(0.5)*
2. **Deparaffinization** — Slides in xylene (3 × 10 min) followed by graded ethanol (100% → 95% → 70%) and rehydration. *(0.5)*
3. **Protease pretreatment** — Pepsin/HCl digestion (typically 0.5 mg/mL pepsin in 0.2 N HCl, 10–30 min at 37 °C) to unmask target DNA by removing cross-linked proteins. *(0.5)*
4. **Denaturation** — Co-denaturation of probe and target DNA at **72 °C for 5 min** on a hybridizer. *(0.5)*
5. **Hybridization** — Incubation at **37 °C for 16–18 hours** in a humidified chamber to allow probe–target annealing. *(0.5)*
6. **Stringent post-hybridization washes** — 0.4× SSC with 0.3% NP-40 at **72 °C** to remove non-specific binding. *(0.5)*
7. **Counterstaining** — Application of **DAPI (blue)** and a coverslip. *(0.5)*
8. **Microscopy and scoring** — Epifluorescence microscope with DAPI, Orange, and Green filter sets; score **≥ 20 tumor nuclei**, record HER2 and CEP17 signals per nucleus, calculate **R = S_HER2/S_CEP17** and $\bar{C}_{HER2} = S_{HER2}/N$. *(1.0)*
9. **Interpretation** — Apply ASCO/CAP 2018 decision rules to report as Positive, Equivocal, or Negative. *(0.5)*

#### (b) Explain the ASCO/CAP 2018 scoring algorithm for HER2 FISH interpretation in breast carcinoma. Tabulate the criteria. **(7 marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**Background (1 mark):**
The American Society of Clinical Oncology and the College of American Pathologists (ASCO/CAP) publish standardized criteria to ensure reproducibility of HER2 FISH reporting. The **2018 update** requires reporting both the **HER2/CEP17 ratio (R)** and the **average HER2 copy number per cell ($\bar{C}_{HER2}$)**.

**Scoring Procedure (2 marks):**
- Score signals in **≥ 20** (preferably 40–60) non-overlapping, non-overlapping invasive tumor nuclei.
- For each nucleus, count **orange (HER2)** and **green (CEP17)** signals.
- Compute:
  - $R = \dfrac{\sum h_i}{\sum c_i} = \dfrac{S_{HER2}}{S_{CEP17}}$
  - $\bar{C}_{HER2} = \dfrac{S_{HER2}}{N}$

**Tabulated ASCO/CAP 2018 Criteria (3 marks):**

| HER2/CEP17 Ratio (R) | Avg HER2 Copy No. ($\bar{C}_{HER2}$) | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| **≥ 2.0** | Any | **POSITIVE** |
| **< 2.0** | **≥ 6.0** | **POSITIVE** |
| **< 2.0** | **≥ 4.0 and < 6.0** | **EQUIVOCAL** (recount / reflex IHC) |
| **< 2.0** | **< 4.0** | **NEGATIVE** |

**Clinical Correlation (1 mark):**
- **Positive** → patient is eligible for **trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DM1, lapatinib**.
- **Equivocal** → re-score by second technologist, or perform **IHC HER2** on the same block.
- **Negative** → standard chemotherapy / endocrine therapy (depending on ER/PR).

---

### **Question B (14 marks) — Option 2**

#### (a) What is HER2? Why is its amplification clinically important? List the tumors in which HER2 testing by FISH is indicated. **(7 marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**HER2 Definition (2 marks):**
HER2 (Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2), encoded by the ***ERBB2* gene** at chromosome **17q12**, is a **185 kDa transmembrane glycoprotein** belonging to the ErbB family of receptor tyrosine kinases. It contains an extracellular ligand-binding domain, a transmembrane segment, and an intracellular tyrosine kinase domain. On dimerization (ligand-induced or constitutive), it activates downstream **RAS-MAPK** and **PI3K-AKT** pathways driving proliferation, survival, and migration.

**Clinical Importance of HER2 Amplification (3 marks):**
- **Prognostic** — Amplified tumors (≈15–20% of breast cancers) are biologically aggressive with worse disease-free and overall survival in untreated patients.
- **Predictive** — Amplification predicts response to **anti-HER2 targeted therapy**, including:
  - **Trastuzumab (Herceptin®)** — humanized monoclonal antibody binding HER2 extracellular domain IV.
  - **Pertuzumab** — antibody binding domain II; complementary to trastuzumab.
  - **Ado-trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1)** — antibody–drug conjugate.
  - **Lapatinib** — small-molecule TKI of HER1/HER2 intracellular domain.
  - **Neratinib, Tucatinib** — newer TKIs.
- **Diagnostic** — Defines a distinct molecular subtype of breast cancer (HER2-enriched).

**Tumors in which HER2 FISH is Indicated (2 marks):**
1. **Invasive breast carcinoma** — primary indication; mandatory before anti-HER2 therapy.
2. **Gastric and gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) adenocarcinoma** — HER2-positive subset benefits from trastuzumab.
3. **Other emerging indications:** salivary duct carcinoma, uterine serous carcinoma, colorectal cancer (less common), urothelial carcinoma.

#### (b) Differentiate between HER2 amplification, HER2 overexpression, polysomy 17, and double-minute chromosomes. How does FISH distinguish among them? **(7 marks)**

**Model Answer:**

**Definitions (4 marks):**

| Term | Definition | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| **HER2 Amplification** | Increase in the **number of copies of the *ERBB2* gene** at 17q12 (focal) | Tandem duplications / *Hsr* (homogeneously staining regions) on chromosome 17 |
| **HER2 Overexpression** | Increased **protein level** of HER2 on the cell membrane (detected by IHC) | Result of gene amplification OR transcriptional upregulation |
| **Polysomy 17** | Gain of **whole extra copies of chromosome 17** | Chromosome 17 non-disjunction → ↑ CEP17 *and* ↑ HER2 |
| **Double-minute chromosomes** | Small, acentric, extrachromosomal circular DNA fragments | Replicating episomes carrying amplified *ERBB2*; seen as **scattered, multiple, small HER2 signals** |

**How FISH Distinguishes Them (3 marks):**

- **Normal:** 2 orange (HER2) + 2 green (CEP17) signals per nucleus → ratio ≈ 1.0
- **Focal HER2 amplification:** 2 green CEP17 + many orange HER2 signals in **tight clusters** → **ratio ≥ 2.0**, classified **POSITIVE**.
- **Polysomy 17:** Both orange and green signals **increase proportionally** → ratio remains near 1.0; classified by **$\bar{C}_{HER2}$** thresholds.
- **Double minutes:** 2 green CEP17 + **numerous scattered small orange dots** distributed throughout the nucleus (not in tight clusters) → ratio ≥ 2.0, classified **POSITIVE**.
- **IHC limitation:** IHC detects *protein* and cannot distinguish amplification from overexpression driven by transcription alone — FISH is the **gold standard** for the *gene-level* determination.

## Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **High-Yield Rapid-Revision Checklist for Module 4 — HER2 FISH**

- ✅ **HER2 gene = *ERBB2* at chromosome 17q12**; encodes a 185 kDa receptor tyrosine kinase.
- ✅ **FISH** = Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization — gold standard for HER2 gene copy number.
- ✅ **Two probes:** **LSI HER2 (orange)** + **CEP17 (green)**.
- ✅ **Always report BOTH:** **HER2/CEP17 ratio (R)** *and* **average HER2 copy number/cell ($\bar{C}_{HER2}$)**.
- ✅ **POSITIVE if:** R ≥ 2.0 (any copy #) **OR** R < 2.0 with $\bar{C}_{HER2}$ ≥ 6.0.
- ✅ **EQUIVOCAL if:** R < 2.0 with $4.0 \leq \bar{C}_{HER2} < 6.0$ → recount or reflex IHC.
- ✅ **NEGATIVE if:** R < 2.0 and $\bar{C}_{HER2}$ < 4.0.
- ✅ **Minimum cells scored = 20** (40–60 preferred).
- ✅ **Heterogeneity:** Report if 5–50% of cells show amplification.
- ✅ **Clinical use:** Guides therapy with **Trastuzumab, Pertuzumab, T-DM1, Lapatinib**.
- ✅ **Indications:** Invasive breast carcinoma; gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma.
- ✅ **ASCO/CAP 2018** is the current guideline version (replaces the older 2013 version).
- ✅ **Equivocal = second scorer + IHC reflex**, not a final FISH result.
- ✅ **FISH ≠ IHC** — IHC measures *protein*, FISH measures *gene copy number*; they are *complementary*, not interchangeable.
- ✅ **Polysomy 17 ≠ amplification** — both probes rise; ratio stays low.
- ✅ **Double minutes** show as scattered, not clustered, HER2 signals.
- ✅ **Stringent wash temperature = 72 °C** in 0.4× SSC + 0.3% NP-40.
- ✅ **Counterstain = DAPI** (blue nuclear stain).
